result:
Done. Shipped to main (`6be86b7`).

## What changed

A full visual overhaul from the cartoony pirate theme to a clean, modern Linear/Duolingo aesthetic across **15 files**.

| Area | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| **Global CSS** | wave/float/glow/sparkle/shake/fog animations | only `fade-in`, `slide-up`, `scale-in`, subtle `streak-pop` (all 0.2s ease-out) |
| **Background** | ocean blue `#060d1f`→blue-900 gradient | slate `#0f172a`→`#1e293b` |
| **Cards** | thick blue/gold borders, solid blocks | glass `bg-white/5 backdrop-blur-sm border-white/10`, `rounded-xl` |
| **Buttons** | gold gradients, `active:scale-95` | emerald primary, `bg-white/10` secondary, ghost text, `transition-all duration-200` |
| **NavBar** | emoji tabs (🗺️💰🏴‍☠️), gold glow | lucide outline icons (Map/BookOpen/User), glass blur, emerald underline |
| **Map** | ocean bg, emoji nodes, dashed rope, waves, stars | slate canvas, numbered/check/lock circles, thin connector line, emerald (Map 1) / violet (Map 2) accents |
| **Heap & Infinite** | gradient cards, sparkle/shake feedback | glass cards, subtle green/red border flash, lucide check/X, clean copy |
| **Dictionary** | "Treasure Chest" 💰 | "Vocabulary" — list with category pills, thin separators |
| **Profile** | "Captain's Quarters" 🏴‍☠️ | "Profile" — glass stat cards ("Heaps complete", "Words learned", "Best streak") |
| **Auth** | starfield/moon/waves, "Set Sail" | clean centered card, thin `Learn`**`BulgEasy`** wordmark |

**Removed:** all pirate language ("plundered", "islands conquered", "voyage"), emoji map icons, gold/treasure palette, starfields, confetti/sparkle/bounce.

## Verification
- `npm run build` — ✅ passes clean (25 routes)
- `npm run lint` — 24 pre-existing `react-hooks` errors remain (ref-access-during-render + setState-in-effect in `HeapGame`/`PWAProvider` logic I didn't touch). I confirmed via `git stash` that the committed version had the **exact same 24** — **zero new lint errors introduced**.

Those pre-existing lint errors are unrelated to styling and out of scope here — want me to fix them in a follow-up?
